---
title: "Scan-to-Defining-Marker Overlap"
---

# Summary
For each target cell type, compares the top-N receptors predicted by each scanning
algorithm against the surface markers experimentally used to define that cell type.
Recall is computed as the fraction of a cell type's defining markers (that were
present in the scanned receptor set) that appear in the top-N predictions. Higher
recall at lower N indicates the algorithm rediscovers biologically meaningful targets.

```{python}
%config InlineBackend.figure_formats = ['svg']
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
from pathlib import Path

from bicytok.imports import importCITE

from figures.scanning_analyses.scan_runners import load_selec_scan_results, load_KL_EMD_scan_results

path_here = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent


# --- Parameters ---
SELEC_SCAN_PATH = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260529_selec_scan_full_bival.csv"
KL_EMD_SCAN_PATH = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260529_KL-EMD_scan_full_expr-match.csv"
MARKERS_CSV_FILE = "CITE-seq_defining_surface_markers.csv"
TOP_N_VALUES = [5, 10, 20, 50, 100]
SIGNAL_RECEPTOR = "Prototype_Signal_Receptor"  # Artificial; excluded from selectivity rankings
CHECK_MARKER_PRESENCE = False  # Warn if defining markers are missing from CITE-seq dataset

# Build cell type → defining marker set from CSV.
# A marker may define multiple cell types; both cell_type_main columns are used.
markers_df = pd.read_csv(path_here / "data" / MARKERS_CSV_FILE)
cell_type_markers: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
for _, row in markers_df.iterrows():
    for col in ["cell_type_main_1", "cell_type_main_2"]:
        ct = str(row[col]).strip()
        if ct and ct != "nan":
            cell_type_markers.setdefault(ct, set()).add(row["surface_marker"])

# Load scan results
opt_selec, _, _, selec_receptors, selec_cell_types = load_selec_scan_results(SELEC_SCAN_PATH)
KL_results, EMD_results, kl_receptors, kl_cell_types = load_KL_EMD_scan_results(KL_EMD_SCAN_PATH)

# Check marker presence in dataset
if CHECK_MARKER_PRESENCE:
    CITE_DF = importCITE()
    available_markers = set(CITE_DF.columns) - {"Cell", "CellType1", "CellType2", "CellType3"}
    for ct, markers in cell_type_markers.items():
        missing = markers - available_markers
        if missing:
            print(f"Warning: For cell type '{ct}', the following defining markers are not in the CITE-seq dataset: {', '.join(missing)}")
```

```{python}
def _rank_receptors(
    metric_3d: np.ndarray,
    receptors: list[str],
    cell_type_idx: int,
    exclude: set[str] | None = None,
) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
    """
    Rank individual receptors by their best score in a 2D receptor-pair matrix.

    For lower-triangular matrices each receptor can appear as either the row or
    the column index; the row-max and column-max are combined so a receptor's
    rank reflects the best pair it participates in regardless of position.
    """
    matrix = metric_3d[:, :, cell_type_idx]
    best = np.fmax(np.nanmax(matrix, axis=1), np.nanmax(matrix, axis=0))
    exclude = exclude or set()
    pairs = [
        (receptors[j], float(best[j]))
        for j in range(len(receptors))
        if not np.isnan(best[j]) and receptors[j] not in exclude
    ]
    return sorted(pairs, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)


def compute_overlap_table(
    metric_3d: np.ndarray,
    receptors: list[str],
    cell_types: list[str],
    cell_type_markers: dict[str, set[str]],
    top_n_values: list[int],
    exclude: set[str] | None = None,
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Compute defining-marker recall at each top-N cutoff for every cell type that
    has annotated defining markers.

    Recall is conditioned on markers that were present in the scanned receptor
    set (markers filtered out before the scan cannot be predicted). The total
    number of defining markers is also recorded for reference.
    """
    scan_receptors = set(receptors) - (exclude or set())
    rows = []
    for i, ct in enumerate(cell_types):
        if ct not in cell_type_markers:
            continue
        defining = cell_type_markers[ct]
        defining_in_scan = defining & scan_receptors
        if not defining_in_scan:
            continue
        ranked_names = [r[0] for r in _rank_receptors(metric_3d, receptors, i, exclude)]
        for n in top_n_values:
            top_n = set(ranked_names[:n])
            n_overlap = len(top_n & defining_in_scan)
            rows.append({
                "cell_type": ct,
                "n": n,
                "recall": n_overlap / len(defining_in_scan),
                "n_overlap": n_overlap,
                "n_defining_in_scan": len(defining_in_scan),
                "n_defining_total": len(defining),
            })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


selec_overlap = compute_overlap_table(
    opt_selec, selec_receptors, selec_cell_types,
    cell_type_markers, TOP_N_VALUES, exclude={SIGNAL_RECEPTOR},
)
kl_overlap = compute_overlap_table(
    KL_results, kl_receptors, kl_cell_types,
    cell_type_markers, TOP_N_VALUES,
)
emd_overlap = compute_overlap_table(
    EMD_results, kl_receptors, kl_cell_types,
    cell_type_markers, TOP_N_VALUES,
)
```

```{python}
# --- Heatmap: recall across cell types and top-N values, one panel per metric ---

all_cell_types = sorted(
    set(selec_overlap["cell_type"]) | set(kl_overlap["cell_type"]) | set(emd_overlap["cell_type"])
)


def _make_pivot(df, cell_types_ordered, top_n_values):
    pivot = df.pivot(index="cell_type", columns="n", values="recall")
    return pivot.reindex(index=cell_types_ordered, columns=top_n_values)


def _make_annot(df, cell_types_ordered, top_n_values):
    """'k/m' strings where k = overlap count, m = defining markers in scan."""
    n_over = df.pivot(index="cell_type", columns="n", values="n_overlap").reindex(
        index=cell_types_ordered, columns=top_n_values
    )
    n_def = df.pivot(index="cell_type", columns="n", values="n_defining_in_scan").reindex(
        index=cell_types_ordered, columns=top_n_values
    )
    annot = pd.DataFrame("", index=cell_types_ordered, columns=top_n_values, dtype=object)
    for ct in cell_types_ordered:
        for n in top_n_values:
            v, d = n_over.loc[ct, n], n_def.loc[ct, n]
            if pd.notna(v) and pd.notna(d):
                annot.loc[ct, n] = f"{int(v)}/{int(d)}"
    return annot


fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(20, 10), sharey=True)

metric_specs = [
    ("Selectivity", selec_overlap),
    ("KL Divergence", kl_overlap),
    ("EMD", emd_overlap),
]

for ax, (title, df) in zip(axes, metric_specs):
    pivot = _make_pivot(df, all_cell_types, TOP_N_VALUES)
    annot = _make_annot(df, all_cell_types, TOP_N_VALUES)
    mask = pivot.isna()

    sns.heatmap(
        pivot,
        mask=mask,
        cmap="Blues",
        vmin=0,
        vmax=1,
        annot=annot,
        fmt="",
        ax=ax,
        cbar=False,
        linewidths=0.5,
        linecolor="lightgray",
        annot_kws={"fontsize": 9},
    )

    # Gray fill for cell types not covered by this metric
    for i, ct in enumerate(all_cell_types):
        for j in range(len(TOP_N_VALUES)):
            if mask.iloc[i, j]:
                ax.add_patch(
                    plt.Rectangle((j, i), 1, 1, fill=True, color="#d0d0d0", zorder=3)
                )

    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", pad=10)
    ax.set_xlabel("Top-N Predicted Receptors", fontsize=12)
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", labelsize=11)
    ax.tick_params(axis="y", labelsize=10, rotation=0)

axes[0].set_ylabel("Cell Type", fontsize=12)

# Shared colorbar
pos0 = axes[0].get_position()
pos2 = axes[2].get_position()
cbar_ax = fig.add_axes([pos2.x1 + 0.01, pos0.y0, 0.015, pos0.height])
norm = plt.Normalize(vmin=0, vmax=1)
sm = plt.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap="Blues", norm=norm)
sm.set_array([])
cbar = fig.colorbar(sm, cax=cbar_ax)
cbar.set_label("Recall (overlap / markers in scan)", fontsize=11)
cbar.ax.tick_params(labelsize=10)

fig.suptitle(
    "Top-N Predicted Receptors vs. Cell-Type Defining Markers\n"
    "(Annotation: predicted overlap / defining markers present in scan)",
    fontsize=13,
    y=1.01,
)
# plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# --- Summary table: per-cell-type marker coverage at top-50 ---
# Shows which markers are reachable by each algorithm and which are missed.

N_SUMMARY = 50

def _top_n_set(metric_3d, receptors, cell_types, ct, n, exclude=None):
    try:
        i = cell_types.index(ct)
    except ValueError:
        return set()
    ranked = _rank_receptors(metric_3d, receptors, i, exclude or set())
    return {r[0] for r in ranked[:n]}


print(f"{'Cell Type':<22} {'N def (scan)':<14} {'Selec':<8} {'KL':<8} {'EMD':<8}  Defining markers in scan")
print("-" * 110)

for ct in all_cell_types:
    defining = cell_type_markers.get(ct, set())
    selec_scan_recs = set(selec_receptors) - {SIGNAL_RECEPTOR}
    kl_scan_recs = set(kl_receptors)
    defining_in_selec = defining & selec_scan_recs
    defining_in_kl = defining & kl_scan_recs

    if not defining_in_selec and not defining_in_kl:
        continue

    top_selec = _top_n_set(opt_selec, selec_receptors, selec_cell_types, ct, N_SUMMARY, exclude={SIGNAL_RECEPTOR})
    top_kl = _top_n_set(KL_results, kl_receptors, kl_cell_types, ct, N_SUMMARY)
    top_emd = _top_n_set(EMD_results, kl_receptors, kl_cell_types, ct, N_SUMMARY)

    n_def_scan = len(defining_in_selec) if defining_in_selec else len(defining_in_kl)
    def_in_scan = defining_in_selec if defining_in_selec else defining_in_kl

    r_selec = f"{len(top_selec & defining_in_selec)}/{len(defining_in_selec)}" if defining_in_selec else "—"
    r_kl = f"{len(top_kl & defining_in_kl)}/{len(defining_in_kl)}" if defining_in_kl else "—"
    r_emd = f"{len(top_emd & defining_in_kl)}/{len(defining_in_kl)}" if defining_in_kl else "—"

    markers_str = ", ".join(sorted(def_in_scan))
    print(f"{ct:<22} {n_def_scan:<14} {r_selec:<8} {r_kl:<8} {r_emd:<8}  {markers_str}")
```
